text: **Penelope:** Anna, thanks for taking me on a {tour|観光ツアー/案内} of DC today.
**Anna:** Sure thing, Penelope.
**Anna:** You are new to town and a {tour|観光/案内} is the best way to see more of the city.
**Penelope:** So, which {tour|ツアー/観光} are we taking?
**Anna:** The one that goes through the city on a bus or the one that goes along the river in a {boat|ボート/船}?
**Anna:** Both!
**Penelope:** What?
**Penelope:** Anna, this isn't going to be one of those {trips|旅行/遠出}, is it?
**Anna:** No.
**Anna:** Follow me.
**Professor Bot:** In this lesson, you are going to hear lots of {prepositions|前置詞}.
**Professor Bot:** What's a preposition, you ask?
**Professor Bot:** It's a word that shows {relationships|関係/つながり} between things.
**Anna:** Anna says they are going on a tour through the city and along the river.
**Anna:** Through and along are both prepositions.
**Anna:** Watch for more.
**Anna:** Well, Penelope, there's our {ride|乗り物/乗車}!
**Penelope:** What?
**Penelope:** Is it?
**Anna:** It's the famous DC {Ducks|特殊な水陸両用車}!
**Anna:** The boat with {wheels|車輪}.
**Anna:** We will ride on the road and then {sail|航行する/進む} on the water
**Penelope:** Who thinks of these things?
**Anna:** I don't know, but I'm glad they do.
**Anna:** Let's get {aboard|乗り込む}!
**Anna:** Hey, did you know that this bus, um, boat, was {created|作られた/生み出された} during World War II to carry people and {supplies|物資/補給品}.
**Anna:** Wow, you know, a tour is so much more {interesting|興味深い} with fun facts like that one.
**Penelope:** I agree.
**Penelope:** I love fun facts.
**Boy:** Did I hear you say you love fun facts?
**Anna:** Yeah.
**Boy:** Well, I have {tons|たくさん} of fun facts for this tour
**Penelope:** Great!
**Anna:** Penelope, where are his {parents|両親}?
**Penelope:** Maybe.
**Anna:** Maybe he's the {captain's|船長の} son.
**Anna:** And he helps his dad on {tours|ツアー}.
**Anna:** Oh yeah.
**Anna:** It would be so {awesome|素晴らしい} to ride around the city in a boat all day with your dad.
**Penelope:** This is amazing, Anna.
**Penelope:** There are so many {beautiful|美しい} buildings along this road.
**Anna:** Hey, the Washington Monument is on the left!
**Professor Bot:** I have a fun fact too.
**Professor Bot:** Did you know that the Washington Monument was {damaged|損傷した} in an earthquake?
**Professor Bot:** Whoa, did you feel that?
**Anna:** I heard around the city in a boat, along this road, on the left, and across from the White House
**Anna:** All of these are {prepositions|前置詞} showing place.
**Anna:** Let's watch for more places and prepositions.
**Anna:** Look, Anna, we're across from the White House.
**Anna:** Penelope, the only thing between us and the president is the street and a park.
**Anna:** And a security gate, and police officers with guns.
**Boy:** Excuse me, do you want to know a fun fact about the White House
**Anna:** Yes.
**Boy:** Inside the White House there is a swimming pool, a movie theater, and {thirty-two|32} bathrooms.
**Penelope:** Thirty-two bathrooms?
**Penelope:** That's a lot of bathrooms.
**Boy:** I'd be happy with two.
**Boy:** I live in a house with my mom, four sisters, two aunts, and only one bathroom.
**Penelope:** Oh my.
**Penelope:** You know, Anna, we should give him a {tip|チップ/助言}.
**Anna:** Of course, it is {polite|丁寧な/礼儀正しい|/pəˈlaɪt/|It is polite to say thank you.} to {tip|チップを渡す/心付けをする|/tɪp/|We tip waiters in restaurants.} your tour guide.
**Anna:** Luckily, I have lots of {dollar|ドル/アメリカの通貨|/ˈdɑːlər/|One dollar is 100 cents.} bills.
**Penelope:** That was a great {fun|楽しい/面白い|/fʌn/|Fun games make children happy.} fact.
**Penelope:** Here you go.
**Boy:** Thank you.
**Boy:** I can tell you fun {facts|事実/情報|/fæks/|Fun facts are interesting.} all day.
**Professor Bot:** Did you hear any more {prepositions|前置詞|/ˌprepəˈzɪʃənz/|Prepositions show location.} of place?
**Anna:** I heard between us and the president, {inside|内側に/中に|/ɪnˈsaɪd/|Inside the room is warm.} the White House, and in a house.
**Anna:** And did you hear that the {duck|アヒル/鴨|/dʌk/|A duck swims in water.} boat can go on the river?
**Anna:** I don't {believe|信じる/思う|/bɪˈliːv/|I believe in you.} it.
**Anna:** Join us next time to find out which {preposition|前置詞|/ˌprepəˈzɪʃən/|A preposition shows relationship.} we will use.
**Anna:** The boat went on the river, or the boat went {into|〜の中へ/〜の内部に|/ˈɪntuː/|She walked into the room.} the river?
